Monte Carlo Gold Price Simulation¶

Using live Gold Futures data (GC=F) from Yahoo Finance

In [8]:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import yfinance as yf
In [9]:
# Fetch live gold futures price
ticker = yf.Ticker('GC=F')
hist = ticker.history(period='1y')

S0 = float(hist['Close'].iloc[-1])
last_date = hist.index[-1].strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
print(f'Gold Futures (GC=F) as of {last_date}: ${S0:,.2f}')

Gold Futures (GC=F) as of 2026-03-06: $5,103.90
In [10]:
# Calculate drift and volatility from historical data
log_returns = np.log(hist['Close'] / hist['Close'].shift(1)).dropna()
mu    = float(log_returns.mean() * 252)
sigma = float(log_returns.std() * np.sqrt(252))

print(f'Annual Drift:      {mu*100:.2f}%')
print(f'Annual Volatility: {sigma*100:.2f}%')

Annual Drift:      55.96%
Annual Volatility: 26.34%
In [11]:
# Run Monte Carlo simulation
T           = 1.0
dt          = 1/252
N           = int(T / dt)
simulations = 1000

np.random.seed(42)
Z = np.random.standard_normal((N, simulations))
daily_returns = np.exp((mu - 0.5 * sigma**2) * dt + sigma * np.sqrt(dt) * Z)

price_paths = np.zeros((N + 1, simulations))
price_paths[0] = S0
for t in range(1, N + 1):
    price_paths[t] = price_paths[t-1] * daily_returns[t-1]

final_prices = price_paths[-1]
print('Simulation complete!')

Simulation complete!
In [12]:
# Summary statistics
mean_price   = np.mean(final_prices)
median_price = np.median(final_prices)
ci_5         = np.percentile(final_prices, 5)
ci_95        = np.percentile(final_prices, 95)
prob_up      = np.mean(final_prices > S0) * 100

print('=' * 45)
print('   Monte Carlo Gold Price Simulation')
print('=' * 45)
print(f'Starting Price:     ${S0:,.2f}')
print(f'Mean Final Price:   ${mean_price:,.2f}')
print(f'Median Final Price: ${median_price:,.2f}')
print(f'5th Percentile:     ${ci_5:,.2f}')
print(f'95th Percentile:    ${ci_95:,.2f}')
print(f'Prob. Price Rises:  {prob_up:.1f}%')
print('=' * 45)

=============================================
   Monte Carlo Gold Price Simulation
=============================================
Starting Price:     $5,103.90
Mean Final Price:   $8,940.76
Median Final Price: $8,669.60
5th Percentile:     $5,603.29
95th Percentile:    $13,393.55
Prob. Price Rises:  97.6%
=============================================
In [13]:
# Plot price paths
days = np.arange(N + 1)
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5))
plt.plot(days, price_paths[:, :200], alpha=0.15, linewidth=0.5, color='gold')
plt.plot(days, np.mean(price_paths, axis=1), color='darkorange', linewidth=2, label='Mean Path')
plt.fill_between(days,
                 np.percentile(price_paths, 5, axis=1),
                 np.percentile(price_paths, 95, axis=1),
                 alpha=0.2, color='orange', label='5th-95th Percentile')
plt.axhline(S0, color='gray', linestyle='--', linewidth=1, label=f'Start: ${S0:,.0f}')
plt.title('Monte Carlo: Gold Futures Price Paths')
plt.xlabel('Trading Days')
plt.ylabel('Gold Price (USD/oz)')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [14]:
# Plot distribution of final prices
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
plt.hist(final_prices, bins=60, color='gold', edgecolor='darkorange', alpha=0.8)
plt.axvline(mean_price,   color='red',    linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5, label=f'Mean: ${mean_price:,.0f}')
plt.axvline(median_price, color='blue',   linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5, label=f'Median: ${median_price:,.0f}')
plt.axvline(ci_5,         color='green',  linestyle=':',  linewidth=1.5, label=f'5th pct: ${ci_5:,.0f}')
plt.axvline(ci_95,        color='purple', linestyle=':',  linewidth=1.5, label=f'95th pct: ${ci_95:,.0f}')
plt.title('Distribution of Final Gold Prices (1 Year)')
plt.xlabel('Gold Price (USD/oz)')
plt.ylabel('Frequency')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
